methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $129M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
INERATEC, a Series B climate and energy technology company that has raised $129M to date, is valued using a comparable universe of 9 peers: 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private firms across the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses the $129M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying $245M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), plus one additional peer—yields a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous technology maturity and capital intensity within climate tech.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $208M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $300M. The $245M stage-progression result anchors the lower bound, while peer median anchors toward the upper range. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within the crowded carbon capture and energy conversion space, and the significant valuation variance observable in the comparable set, which suggests stage and sector alone incompletely explain pricing in this domain.
